rationale: Earth & Environmental Sciences spans solid-earth geology to climate models to ocean biology to sustainability policy. Thirteen subspecialties split by Earth system (atmosphere, hydrosphere, lithosphere, biosphere) plus cross-cutting topics (climate, sustainability, hazards). I separated Climate Science from Atmospheric Science even though they overlap — climate has its own identity (and journals, conferences) post-IPCC. Sustainability Science gets its own card because solutions-oriented work (transitions, energy systems, urban) is a distinct community from physical environmental science.

subspecialties.name: Climate Science
covers: climate dynamics, climate modeling (CMIP-class), paleoclimate, climate sensitivity, climate attribution, regional climate
Nat Clim Change, J Climate, Clim Dyn
explicitly_excludes: climate policy → Public Health or Social Sciences

subspecialties.name: Atmospheric Science
covers: weather and forecasting, atmospheric dynamics, aerosols, cloud microphysics, atmospheric chemistry transport, boundary layer
JAS, MWR, Atmos Chem Phys

subspecialties.name: Oceanography
covers: physical oceanography (currents, overturning), biological oceanography, ocean biogeochemistry, deep-sea, sea ice, ENSO-ocean
J Geophys Res Oceans, Ocean Sci

subspecialties.name: Hydrology & Water Resources
covers: surface water hydrology, groundwater, water quality, drought science, flood modeling, snow hydrology, water resources management
Water Resour Res, HESS, J Hydrol
explicitly_excludes: civil water engineering → Mechanical & Aerospace Engineering

subspecialties.name: Geology & Tectonics
subspecialties.tagline: Plate tectonics, structural geology, petrology, sedimentology.
covers: structural geology, tectonics, petrology, mineralogy, sedimentology, stratigraphy, geochronology, geomorphology
GSA Bull, Earth Planet Sci Lett, Geology

subspecialties.name: Geophysics & Seismology
covers: seismology, earthquake physics, Earth interior structure, geomagnetism, gravity, exploration geophysics, seismic hazard

subspecialties.name: Geochemistry & Mineralogy
subspecialties.tagline: Element cycling, isotopes, crystal chemistry, geobiology.
covers: isotope geochemistry, low- and high-temperature geochemistry, mineralogy, crystal chemistry, geobiology, biogeochemistry

subspecialties.name: Ecosystem Science & Biogeochemistry
subspecialties.tagline: Carbon cycle, nitrogen cycle, ecosystem flux, terrestrial.
covers: carbon and nitrogen cycling, ecosystem ecology, eddy covariance and flux research, terrestrial biogeochemistry, ecosystem models
Glob Change Biol, Biogeosci, Ecosystems

subspecialties.name: Remote Sensing & Earth Observation
subspecialties.tagline: Satellites, drones, hyperspectral, ML for Earth obs.
covers: satellite remote sensing, hyperspectral imaging, UAV remote sensing, ML for Earth observation, SAR, GNSS, land surface mapping
Remote Sens Environ, IEEE TGRS
explicitly_excludes: computer vision methods → Computer Science & AI

subspecialties.name: Environmental Engineering & Pollution Control
subspecialties.tagline: Wastewater, air pollution control, remediation, circular.
covers: wastewater treatment, air pollution control, soil remediation, solid waste management, circular economy environmental engineering

subspecialties.name: Sustainability & Transition Science
subspecialties.tagline: Energy transition, sustainable development, urban systems.
covers: energy systems and transitions, sustainable development goals research, urban sustainability, food-energy-water nexus, industrial ecology, LCA
Nat Sustain, Environ Sci Tech sustain
explicitly_excludes: climate policy → Public Health or Social Sciences

subspecialties.name: Natural Hazards & Disaster Science
subspecialties.tagline: Earthquakes, hurricanes, wildfires, risk and resilience.
covers: earthquake hazard, tropical cyclones, wildfire science, volcanic hazards, landslides, multi-hazard risk, disaster resilience science
explicitly_excludes: disaster public health response → Public Health

subspecialties.name: Polar & Cryospheric Science
subspecialties.tagline: Ice sheets, sea ice, permafrost, polar ecosystems.
covers: Antarctic and Arctic science, ice sheet dynamics, sea ice physics and observation, permafrost, snow science, polar oceanography overlap

uncovered_concerns: Considered Volcanology as its own card — folds under Geology & Tectonics + Natural Hazards comfortably.
Considered Paleontology — folds under Ecology & Evolution at level 1 (already there), not here.
Considered Atmospheric Sciences vs. Climate as one merged card — kept them split because the working communities self-identify differently.
